question: Where is the surrounding context for autocommit and close?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00231] documentation_set: Commit any pending transaction to the database. If autocommit is True , or there is no open transaction, this method does nothing. If autocommit is False , a new transaction is implicitly opened if a pending transaction was committed by this method.
[D00232] documentation_set: rollback ( ) ¶  Roll back to the start of any pending transaction. If autocommit is True , or there is no open transaction, this method does nothing. If autocommit is False , a new transaction is implicitly opened if a pending transaction was rolled back by this method.
[D00233] documentation_set: close ( ) ¶  Close the database connection. If autocommit is False , any pending transaction is implicitly rolled back. If autocommit is True or LEGACY_TRANSACTION_CONTROL , no implicit transaction control is executed. Make sure to commit() before closing to avoid losing pending changes.
[D00234] documentation_set: execute ( sql , parameters = () , / ) ¶  Create a new Cursor object and call
[D00235] documentation_set: execute() on it with the given sql and parameters . Return the new cursor object.
[D00236] documentation_set: executemany ( sql , parameters , / ) ¶  Create a new Cursor object and call

[D00233] documentation_set: close ( ) ¶  Close the database connection. If autocommit is False , any pending transaction is implicitly rolled back. [D00182] documentation_set: sqlite3. paramstyle ¶  String constant stating the type of parameter marker formatting expected by the sqlite3 module. Required by the DB-API. Hard-coded to
[D00184] documentation_set: The named DB-API parameter style is also supported.  sqlite3. sqlite_version ¶
[D00186] documentation_set: Version number of the runtime SQLite library as a tuple of  integers .
[D00187] documentation_set: sqlite3. threadsafety ¶  Integer constant required by the DB-API 2.0, stating the level of thread safety the sqlite3 module supports. This attribute is set based on the default threading mode the underlying SQLite library is compiled with. The SQLite threading modes are:
[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.
[D00191] documentation_set: The mappings from SQLite threading modes to DB-API 2.0 threadsafety levels are as follows:

[D00217] documentation_set: How to use the connection context manager  Changed in version 3.13: A ResourceWarning is emitted if close() is not called before a Connection object is deleted.

question: Where is the surrounding context for transaction and autocommit?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00229] documentation_set: The blob size cannot be changed using the Blob class. Use the SQL function zeroblob to create a blob with a fixed size.
[D00231] documentation_set: Commit any pending transaction to the database. If autocommit is True , or there is no open transaction, this method does nothing. If autocommit is False , a new transaction is implicitly opened if a pending transaction was committed by this method.
[D00232] documentation_set: rollback ( ) ¶  Roll back to the start of any pending transaction. If autocommit is True , or there is no open transaction, this method does nothing. If autocommit is False , a new transaction is implicitly opened if a pending transaction was rolled back by this method.
[D00233] documentation_set: close ( ) ¶  Close the database connection. If autocommit is False , any pending transaction is implicitly rolled back. If autocommit is True or LEGACY_TRANSACTION_CONTROL , no implicit transaction control is executed. Make sure to commit() before closing to avoid losing pending changes.
[D00234] documentation_set: execute ( sql , parameters = () , / ) ¶  Create a new Cursor object and call

[D00218] documentation_set: An SQLite database connection has the following attributes and methods:  cursor ( factory = Cursor ) ¶
[D00219] documentation_set: Create and return a Cursor object. The cursor method accepts a single optional parameter factory . If supplied, this must be a callable returning an instance of Cursor or its subclasses.

[D00225] documentation_set: name ( str ) – The name of the database where the blob is located. Defaults to "main" .
[D00226] documentation_set: Raises :  OperationalError – When trying to open a blob in a WITHOUT

question: Where is the surrounding context for argument and first?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00349] documentation_set: authorizer_callback to be invoked for each attempt to access a column of a table in the database. The callback should return one of SQLITE_OK ,
[D00351] documentation_set: The first argument to the callback signifies what kind of operation is to be authorized. The second and third argument will be arguments or None
[D00352] documentation_set: depending on the first argument. The 4th argument is the name of the database (“main”, “temp”, etc.) if applicable. The 5th argument is the name of the inner-most trigger or view that is responsible for the access attempt or
[D00353] documentation_set: None if this access attempt is directly from input SQL code.  Please consult the SQLite documentation about the possible values for the first argument and the meaning of the second and third argument depending on the first one. All necessary constants are available in the sqlite3 module.
[D00354] documentation_set: Passing None as authorizer_callback will disable the authorizer.  Changed in version 3.11: Added support for disabling the authorizer using None .
[D00355] documentation_set: Changed in version 3.13: Passing authorizer_callback as a keyword argument is deprecated. The parameter will become positional-only in Python 3.15.

question: Where is the surrounding context for transaction and autocommit?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00229] documentation_set: The blob size cannot be changed using the Blob class. Use the SQL function zeroblob to create a blob with a fixed size.
[D00231] documentation_set: Commit any pending transaction to the database. If autocommit is True , or there is no open transaction, this method does nothing. If autocommit is False , a new transaction is implicitly opened if a pending transaction was committed by this method.
[D00232] documentation_set: rollback ( ) ¶  Roll back to the start of any pending transaction. If autocommit is True , or there is no open transaction, this method does nothing. If autocommit is False , a new transaction is implicitly opened if a pending transaction was rolled back by this method.
[D00233] documentation_set: close ( ) ¶  Close the database connection. If autocommit is False , any pending transaction is implicitly rolled back. If autocommit is True or LEGACY_TRANSACTION_CONTROL , no implicit transaction control is executed. Make sure to commit() before closing to avoid losing pending changes.

[D00105] documentation_set: Open a connection to an SQLite database.  Parameters :
[D00106] documentation_set: database ( path-like object ) – The path to the database file to be opened. You can pass ":memory:" to create an SQLite database existing only in memory , and open a connection to it.

[D00145] documentation_set: connect() to look up a converter function using the declared types for each column. The types are declared when the database table is created.
[D00146] documentation_set: sqlite3 will look up a converter function using the first word of the declared type as the converter dictionary key. For example:
[D00165] documentation_set: This flag may be combined with PARSE_COLNAMES using the |  (bitwise or) operator.
[D00166] documentation_set: Note  Generated fields (for example MAX(p) ) are returned as str . Use PARSE_COLNAMES to enforce types for such queries.
[D00167] documentation_set: sqlite3. PARSE_COLNAMES ¶  Pass this flag value to the detect_types parameter of
[D00168] documentation_set: connect() to look up a converter function by using the type name, parsed from the query column name, as the converter dictionary key. The query column name must be wrapped in double quotes ( " ) and the type name must be wrapped in square brackets ( [] ).
[D00175] documentation_set: This flag may be combined with PARSE_DECLTYPES using the |  (bitwise or) operator.

[D00393] documentation_set: Return an iterator to dump the database as SQL source code. Useful when saving an in-memory database for later restoration. Similar to the .dump command in the sqlite3 shell.
[D00394] documentation_set: Parameters :  filter ( str | None ) – An optional LIKE pattern for database objects to dump, e.g. prefix_% . If None (the default), all database objects will be included.
[D00395] documentation_set: Example:  # Convert file example.db to SQL dump file dump.sql
[D00405] documentation_set: Changed in version 3.13: Added the filter parameter.  backup ( target , * , pages = -1 , progress = None , name = 'main' , sleep = 0.250 ) ¶
[D00406] documentation_set: Create a backup of an SQLite database.  Works even if the database is being accessed by other clients or concurrently by the same connection.
[D00407] documentation_set: Parameters :  target ( Connection ) – The database connection to save the backup to.
[D00408] documentation_set: pages ( int ) – The number of pages to copy at a time. If equal to or less than 0 , the entire database is copied in a single step. Defaults to -1 .

question: Where is the surrounding context for threads and multiple?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00187] documentation_set: sqlite3. threadsafety ¶  Integer constant required by the DB-API 2.0, stating the level of thread safety the sqlite3 module supports. This attribute is set based on the default threading mode the underlying SQLite library is compiled with. The SQLite threading modes are:
[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.
[D00191] documentation_set: The mappings from SQLite threading modes to DB-API 2.0 threadsafety levels are as follows:
[D00192] documentation_set: SQLite threading mode  threadsafety

[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.
[D00191] documentation_set: The mappings from SQLite threading modes to DB-API 2.0 threadsafety levels are as follows:
[D00192] documentation_set: SQLite threading mode  threadsafety

[D00395] documentation_set: Example:  # Convert file example.db to SQL dump file dump.sql
[D00405] documentation_set: Changed in version 3.13: Added the filter parameter.  backup ( target , * , pages = -1 , progress = None , name = 'main' , sleep = 0.250 ) ¶
[D00406] documentation_set: Create a backup of an SQLite database.  Works even if the database is being accessed by other clients or concurrently by the same connection.
[D00407] documentation_set: Parameters :  target ( Connection ) – The database connection to save the backup to.
[D00408] documentation_set: pages ( int ) – The number of pages to copy at a time. If equal to or less than 0 , the entire database is copied in a single step. Defaults to -1 .
[D00409] documentation_set: progress ( callback | None) – If set to a callable , it is invoked with three integer arguments for every backup iteration: the status of the last iteration, the remaining number of pages still to be copied, and the total number of pages. Defaults to None .

[D00114] documentation_set: factory ( Connection ) – A custom subclass of Connection to create the connection with, if not the default Connection class.
[D00115] documentation_set: cached_statements ( int ) – The number of statements that sqlite3  should internally cache for this connection, to avoid parsing overhead. By default, 128 statements.
[D00116] documentation_set: uri ( bool ) – If set to True , database is interpreted as a  URI with a file path and an optional query string. The scheme part must be "file:" , and the path can be relative or absolute. The query string allows passing parameters to SQLite, enabling various How to work with SQLite URIs .
[D00117] documentation_set: autocommit ( bool ) – Control PEP 249 transaction handling behaviour. See Connection.autocommit and
[D00118] documentation_set: Transaction control via the autocommit attribute for more information.  autocommit currently defaults to
[D00119] documentation_set: LEGACY_TRANSACTION_CONTROL . The default will change to False in a future Python release.

question: Where is the surrounding context for threads and connection?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00187] documentation_set: sqlite3. threadsafety ¶  Integer constant required by the DB-API 2.0, stating the level of thread safety the sqlite3 module supports. This attribute is set based on the default threading mode the underlying SQLite library is compiled with. The SQLite threading modes are:
[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.
[D00191] documentation_set: The mappings from SQLite threading modes to DB-API 2.0 threadsafety levels are as follows:

[D00187] documentation_set: sqlite3. threadsafety ¶  Integer constant required by the DB-API 2.0, stating the level of thread safety the sqlite3 module supports. This attribute is set based on the default threading mode the underlying SQLite library is compiled with. The SQLite threading modes are:
[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.
[D00191] documentation_set: The mappings from SQLite threading modes to DB-API 2.0 threadsafety levels are as follows:

question: Where is the surrounding context for argument and become?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00357] documentation_set: progress_handler to be invoked for every n  instructions of the SQLite virtual machine. This is useful if you want to get called from SQLite during long-running operations, for example to update a GUI.
[D00358] documentation_set: If you want to clear any previously installed progress handler, call the method with None for progress_handler .
[D00359] documentation_set: Returning a non-zero value from the handler function will terminate the currently executing query and cause it to raise a DatabaseError
[D00360] documentation_set: exception.  Changed in version 3.13: Passing progress_handler as a keyword argument is deprecated. The parameter will become positional-only in Python 3.15.
[D00361] documentation_set: set_trace_callback ( trace_callback ) ¶  Register callable
[D00362] documentation_set: trace_callback to be invoked for each SQL statement that is actually executed by the SQLite backend.

SQLite extensions can define new functions, aggregates or whole new virtual table implementations. One well-known extension is the fulltext-sea...
[D00371] documentation_set: Note  The sqlite3 module is not built with loadable extension support by default, because some platforms (notably macOS) have SQLite libraries which are compiled without this feature. [D00372] documentation_set: Raises an auditing event  sqlite3.enable_load_extension with arguments connection , enabled .

question: Where is the surrounding context for single and thread?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00186] documentation_set: Version number of the runtime SQLite library as a tuple of  integers .
[D00187] documentation_set: sqlite3. threadsafety ¶  Integer constant required by the DB-API 2.0, stating the level of thread safety the sqlite3 module supports. This attribute is set based on the default threading mode the underlying SQLite library is compiled with. The SQLite threading modes are:
[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.

[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.

[D00187] documentation_set: sqlite3. threadsafety ¶  Integer constant required by the DB-API 2.0, stating the level of thread safety the sqlite3 module supports. This attribute is set based on the default threading mode the underlying SQLite library is compiled with. The SQLite threading modes are:
[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.

question: Where is the surrounding context for argument and callback?
title: documentation_set
answer_text:
[D00346] documentation_set: Remove a collation function by setting callable to None .  Changed in version 3.11: The collation name can contain any Unicode character. Earlier, only ASCII characters were allowed.
[D00347] documentation_set: interrupt ( ) ¶  Call this method from a different thread to abort any queries that might be executing on the connection. Aborted queries will raise an OperationalError .
[D00348] documentation_set: set_authorizer ( authorizer_callback ) ¶  Register callable
[D00349] documentation_set: authorizer_callback to be invoked for each attempt to access a column of a table in the database. The callback should return one of SQLITE_OK ,
[D00351] documentation_set: The first argument to the callback signifies what kind of operation is to be authorized. The second and third argument will be arguments or None
[D00352] documentation_set: depending on the first argument. The 4th argument is the name of the database (“main”, “temp”, etc.) if applicable. The 5th argument is the name of the inner-most trigger or view that is responsible for the access attempt or
[D00353] documentation_set: None if this access attempt is directly from input SQL code.  Please consult the SQLite documentation about the possible values for the first argument and the meaning of the second and third argument depending on the first one. All necessary constants are available in the sqlite3 module.
[D00354] documentation_set: Passing None as authorizer_callback will disable the authorizer.  Changed in version 3.11: Added support for disabling the authorizer using None .

question: Where is the surrounding context for argument and authorizer?

answer_text:
[D00349] documentation_set: authorizer_callback to be invoked for each attempt to access a column of a table in the database. The callback should return one of SQLITE_OK ,
[D00351] documentation_set: The first argument to the callback signifies what kind of operation is to be authorized. The second and third argument will be arguments or None
[D00352] documentation_set: depending on the first argument. The 4th argument is the name of the database (“main”, “temp”, etc.) if applicable. The 5th argument is the name of the inner-most trigger or view that is responsible for the access attempt or
[D00353] documentation_set: None if this access attempt is directly from input SQL code.  Please consult the SQLite documentation about the possible values for the first argument and the meaning of the second and third argument depending on the first one. All necessary constants are available in the sqlite3 module.
[D00354] documentation_set: Passing None as authorizer_callback will disable the authorizer.  Changed in version 3.11: Added support for disabling the authorizer using None .
[D00355] documentation_set: Changed in version 3.13: Passing authorizer_callback as a keyword argument is deprecated. The parameter will become positional-only in Python 3.15.
[D00356] documentation_set: set_progress_handler ( progress_handler , n ) ¶  Register callable
[D00357] documentation_set: progress_handler to be invoked for every n  instructions of the SQLite virtual machine. This is useful if you want to get called from SQLite during long-running operations, for example to update a GUI.

[D00352] documentation_set: depending on the first argument. The 4th argument is the name of the database (“main”, “temp”, etc.) if applicable. The 5th argument is the name of the inner-most trigger or view that is responsible for the access attempt or
[D00353] documentation_set: None if this access attempt is directly from input SQL code.  Please consult the SQLite documentation about the possible values for the first argument and the meaning of the second and third argument depending on the first one. All necessary constants are available in the sqlite3 module.
[D00354] documentation_set: Passing None as authorizer_callback will disable the authorizer.  Changed in version 3.11: Added support for disabling the authorizer using None .

[D00356] documentation_set: set_progress_handler ( progress_handler , n ) ¶  Register callable
[D00357] documentation_set: progress_handler to be invoked for every n  instructions of the SQLite virtual machine. This is useful if you want to get called from SQLite during long-running operations, for example to update a GUI.

[D00105] documentation_set: Open a connection to an SQLite database.  Parameters :
[D00106] documentation_set: database ( path-like object ) – The path to the database file to be opened. You can pass ":memory:" to create an SQLite database existing only in memory , and open a connection to it.
[D00107] documentation_set: timeout ( float ) – How many seconds the connection should wait before raising an OperationalError when a table is locked. If another connection opens a transaction to modify a table, that table will be locked until the transaction is committed. Default five seconds.
[D00108] documentation_set: detect_types ( int ) – Control whether and how data types not  natively supported by SQLite
[D00109] documentation_set: are looked up to be converted to Python types, using the converters registered with register_converter() . Set it to any combination (using | , bitwise or) of
[D00110] documentation_set: PARSE_DECLTYPES and PARSE_COLNAMES  to enable this. Column names take precedence over declared types if both flags are set. By default ( 0 ), type detection is disabled.

question: Where is the surrounding context for serialized and sqlite?
title: documentation_set

[D00188] documentation_set: Single-thread : In this mode, all mutexes are disabled and SQLite is unsafe to use in more than a single thread at once.
[D00189] documentation_set: Multi-thread : In this mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads provided that no single database connection is used simultaneously in two or more threads.
[D00190] documentation_set: Serialized : In serialized mode, SQLite can be safely used by multiple threads with no restriction.
[D00191] documentation_set: The mappings from SQLite threading modes to DB-API 2.0 threadsafety levels are as follows:
[D00192] documentation_set: SQLite threading mode  threadsafety
[D00197] documentation_set: 2  Threads may share the module, but not connections

[D00406] documentation_set: Create a backup of an SQLite database.  Works even if the database is being accessed by other clients or concurrently by the same connection.
[D00407] documentation_set: Parameters :  target ( Connection ) – The database connection to save the backup to.
[D00408] documentation_set: pages ( int ) – The number of pages to copy at a time. If equal to or less than 0 , the entire database is copied in a single step. Defaults to -1 .
[D00409] documentation_set: progress ( callback | None) – If set to a callable , it is invoked with three integer arguments for every backup iteration: the status of the last iteration, the remaining number of pages still to be copied, and the total number of pages. Defaults to None .
[D00410] documentation_set: name ( str ) – The name of the database to back up. Either "main" (the default) for the main database,
[D00411] documentation_set: "temp" for the temporary database, or the name of a custom database as attached using the
[D00413] documentation_set: sleep ( float ) – The number of seconds to sleep between successive attempts to back up remaining pages.
[D00414] documentation_set: Example 1, copy an existing database into another:  def

[D00407] documentation_set: Parameters :  target ( Connection ) – The database connection to save the backup to.
[D00408] documentation_set: pages ( int ) – The number of pages to copy at a time. If equal to or less than 0 , the entire database is copied in a single step. Defaults to -1 .
[D00409] documentation_set: progress ( callback | None) – If set to a callable , it is invoked with three integer arguments for every backup iteration: the status of the last iteration, the remaining number of pages still to be copied, and the total number of pages. Defaults to None .

[D00220] documentation_set: blobopen ( table , column , rowid , / , * , readonly = False , name = 'main' ) ¶  Open a Blob handle to an existing
[D00224] documentation_set: rowid ( int ) – The row id where the blob is located.  readonly ( bool ) – Set to True if the blob should be opened without write permissions. Defaults to False .
[D00225] documentation_set: name ( str ) – The name of the database where the blob is located. Defaults to "main" .
[D00226] documentation_set: Raises :  OperationalError – When trying to open a blob in a WITHOUT
[D00229] documentation_set: The blob size cannot be changed using the Blob class. Use the SQL function zeroblob to create a blob with a fixed size.
